title: Leftover Chocolate Hot Chocolate
summary: This indulgent hot choc is the perfect way to use up leftover chocolates after Christmas.

method.description: Pour about 60% of your double cream into a mixing bowl. Use a whisk to lightly whip it until it is thickened and glossy with very soft peaks. Set aside in a cool place.
Roughly chop up your leftover chocolates.

Place the milk, icing sugar and remaining double cream in a saucepan and gently warm over a medium heat. Add in the chocolate and reduce to a low heat. Stir for a couple of minutes until the chocolate has all melted and emulsified to create your hot chocolate. It should be glossy and a similar consistency to double cream.
Pour the hot chocolate into mugs and top each with a spoon of the whipped cream.

chefs.name: Ayala Daly
chefs.summary: Ayala Daly is Mob's content producer and resident vibe bringer. You’ve probably seen her all over the stories but she’s no stranger to the kitchen. Ayala blends her background as a food researcher with her passion for cooking. With a knack for experimentation, she crafts easy-to-follow recipes bursting with bold flavours, drawing inspiration from regular trips to her favourite farms and the thrill of all things spicy.
